text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The main goal is to make the code cleaner, easier to read, and simpler to maintain. When you refactor, you do not add new features or fix bugs; you simply rearrange and rewrite parts of the code to make it more logical and efficient. This practice helps reduce technical debt, which is the extra work created by messy or poorly organized code. Over time, regular refactoring saves time and effort by making the codebase easier to understand and modify for future projects.
